property: Bootstrap zip reproducibility — two builds at the same pin produce byte-identical output
verified_in_session: v1-prep continuation of M6 close (commit d652baa pin refresh + this re-verification)

implications.f_droid_reproducibility: F-Droid's reproducible-build verification will succeed on this snapshot — anyone rebuilding from this commit + the pinned snapshot will get the same SHA256.
implications.supply_chain_integrity: If a future build at this pin produces a different hash, that's evidence of a build-environment change OR a script regression — not silent upstream drift (the pin gates against that).

reproducibility_techniques_in_place: find | sort | zip -@ stabilizes zip-entry order
SYMLINKS.txt sidecar entries are sorted before zip creation
Upstream snapshot hash (Packages.aarch64 || Packages.all) is pinned via tools/scripts/m4-bootstrap-snapshot.sha256
Per-package .deb sha256 verification on download (transitively pins all 1300+ binaries)
next_drift_handling: When upstream Termux apt repo evolves, build-bootstrap.sh exits 1 with a clear 'snapshot drift detected' message. Re-run with UPDATE_SNAPSHOT=1 to accept + commit the new pin. Documented in .omc/v1-release-kickoff.md §5.
